---
title: "Figure 4 — the score across the 5 macro algorithms: bias, per-interval and accumulated information"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
SCORE-LEVEL Bartlett tests, time-resolved, for the 5-algorithm comparison. Reads the same
5 ensemble CSVs figures/data/figure_3_time_dlik_{NR,R,NMR,MR,IR}.csv (each ~1 GB, 1000 sims;
cell N_ch=100, Current_Noise=1e-4, interval=0.1 tau) ONCE each, filters to the per-interval
score and the predictive-moment derivatives for k_off, then discards.

The per-interval score is the STORED d logL_t / d k_off (component_path=="logL",
calculus=="derivative", param_index==1). Verified against the analytic Gaussian score
  s_t = (y_t - mu_t)/v_t * dmu_t + 0.5*((y_t-mu_t)^2/v_t^2 - 1/v_t) * dv_t
to machine precision (ratio 1.0000 at a sampled interval). The per-interval Fisher is the
Gaussian FIM diagonal
  F_t = dmu_t^2 / v_t + 0.5 * dv_t^2 / v_t^2 ,
verified to sum over the recording to the stored total Gaussian_Fisher_Information[k_off,k_off]
(sum_t F_t = 9447.95 exactly for the first recording). (mu_t = y_mean, v_t = y_var.)

Three rows, all time-resolved, all with the agonist shading; columns are the five approximations.
  A  score bias: sbar_t = mean over recordings of s_t. Test 1, E[score]=0. null line 0.
  B  per-interval information ratio: Var_recordings(s_t) / mean_recordings(F_t). Test 2 at the
     interval level. null line 1. This is the CONTROL: per step the identity holds for all five.
  C  accumulated information ratio: Var_recordings(sum_{k<=t} s_k) / sum_{k<=t} mean_recordings(F_k)
     = J_T / F_T. Test 2 at the recording level. null line 1. The naive pair accumulates the
     temporal correlation of the score and diverges from 1 (the overconfidence of Figure 2); the
     recursive trio stays near 1. This is the whole argument: the identity holds per interval (B)
     but fails once the correlated score is summed (C).

J_T is the variance ACROSS the 1000 recordings of the progressive (cumulative) score; F_T is the
cumulative sum of the per-interval Fisher. Rows A and C use a separate scale per block (naive
NR/NMR | recursive R/MR/IR); row B is shared (all near 1). B and C confidence bands are a
nonparametric bootstrap over the 1000 recordings; row A is mean +/- 1.96 SE.

RENDER: this reads 5 GB, too heavy for a laptop; run where figures/data/ resolves.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r read}
ALGOS  <- c("NR", "NMR", "R", "MR", "IR")   # non-recursive | recursive  (NMR = data-file key)
ALAB   <- c(NR = "NR", NMR = "MNR", R = "R", MR = "MR", IR = "IR")   # display label
FULL   <- c(NR = "Non-Recursive", NMR = "Mean Non-Recursive", R = "Recursive",
            MR = "Mean Recursive", IR = "Interval Recursive")
P_MAIN <- 1L    # k_off (param_index); the score is essentially parameter-independent
B      <- 400   # bootstrap replicates over recordings

read_one <- function(a) {
  dt <- fread(sprintf("../data/figure_3_time_dlik_%s.csv", a), skip = 1, showProgress = FALSE)
  pick <- function(comp, calc, p = NA) {
    q <- dt[component_path == comp & calculus == calc & !is.na(sample_index)]
    if (!is.na(p)) q <- q[param_index == p]
    # LOAD-BEARING dedup: the writer emits every evolution row TWICE (one
    # segment_index-blank copy with no timing + one segment_index=0 copy with
    # timing), identical values. Collapse to one row per (sim,sample) so the
    # dcast in compute_one has a single value per cell. (== filtering segment_index==0.)
    q[, .(v = mean(value)), by = .(simulation_index, sample_index)]
  }
  sc <- pick("logL",   "derivative", P_MAIN); setnames(sc, "v", "s")
  dm <- pick("y_mean", "derivative", P_MAIN); setnames(dm, "v", "dm")
  dv <- pick("y_var",  "derivative", P_MAIN); setnames(dv, "v", "dv")
  yv <- pick("y_var",  "primitive");          setnames(yv, "v", "yv")
  tm <- unique(dt[!is.na(patch_current), .(sample_index, t = step_middle, agonist)])
  tm <- tm[, .(t = first(t), agonist = first(agonist)), by = sample_index]
  rm(dt); gc(verbose = FALSE)
  X <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = c("simulation_index", "sample_index")),
              list(sc, dm, dv, yv))
  X[, I := dm^2 / yv + 0.5 * dv^2 / yv^2]           # per-interval Gaussian FIM (k_off)
  list(si = X[, .(algo = a, simulation_index, sample_index, s, I)], tm = tm)
}
raw  <- lapply(ALGOS, read_one)
SI   <- rbindlist(lapply(raw, `[[`, "si"))
tmap <- raw[[1]]$tm
tmap[, t := t * 1000]                     # step_middle seconds -> ms (coherent with Figure 1)
setorder(SI, algo, simulation_index, sample_index)
```

```{r compute}
set.seed(1)
colVar    <- function(M) { n <- nrow(M); m <- colMeans(M); (colMeans(M * M) - m^2) * n / (n - 1) }
rowCumsum <- function(M) t(apply(M, 1, cumsum))   # recordings x samples cumulative score

compute_one <- function(d) {
  Sm  <- as.matrix(dcast(d, simulation_index ~ sample_index, value.var = "s")[, -1])
  Im  <- as.matrix(dcast(d, simulation_index ~ sample_index, value.var = "I")[, -1])
  smp <- as.integer(colnames(Sm)); n <- nrow(Sm)
  vA  <- colVar(Sm)
  mA  <- colMeans(Sm)                                   # A: score mean
  rB  <- vA / colMeans(Im)                              # B: per-interval Var(s)/F
  rC  <- colVar(rowCumsum(Sm)) / cumsum(colMeans(Im))   # C: cumulative J_T/F_T
  repB <- matrix(NA_real_, B, length(smp))
  repC <- matrix(NA_real_, B, length(smp))
  for (b in seq_len(B)) {
    idx <- sample.int(n, n, replace = TRUE)
    Sb  <- Sm[idx, , drop = FALSE]; Ib <- Im[idx, , drop = FALSE]
    repB[b, ] <- colVar(Sb) / colMeans(Ib)
    repC[b, ] <- colVar(rowCumsum(Sb)) / cumsum(colMeans(Ib))
  }
  ci <- function(M, p) apply(M, 2, quantile, p, na.rm = TRUE)
  data.table(sample_index = smp,
             mA = mA, seA = sqrt(vA / n), Fbar = colMeans(Im),
             rB = rB, rB_lo = ci(repB, .025), rB_hi = ci(repB, .975),
             rC = rC, rC_lo = ci(repC, .025), rC_hi = ci(repC, .975))
}
RES <- SI[, compute_one(.SD), by = algo]
RES <- merge(RES, tmap, by = "sample_index")
RES[, algo := factor(algo, levels = ALGOS)]

dA <- RES[is.finite(mA)]
# B is a ratio with denominator F_t; restrict to informative intervals (F_t appreciable)
# per algorithm, else it blows up where F_t -> 0 (pre/post pulse, no information).
# rB_lo > 0 / rC_lo > 0: the log10 axis needs strictly positive ribbon bounds.
dB <- RES[is.finite(rB) & is.finite(rB_lo) & rB_lo > 0]
dB <- dB[, .SD[Fbar > 1e-3 * max(Fbar, na.rm = TRUE)], by = algo]
dC <- RES[is.finite(rC) & is.finite(rC_lo) & rC_lo > 0]
```

```{r layout}
COL <- "#0072B2"; NUL <- "#009E73"
naive <- c("NR", "NMR"); recg <- c("R", "MR", "IR")

grange <- function(lo, hi, algo, pad = .08) {
  g <- tibble(lo, hi, grp = ifelse(algo %in% naive, 1L, 2L)) %>% group_by(grp) %>%
    summarise(lo = min(lo), hi = max(hi), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(pd = (hi - lo) * pad, lo = lo - pd, hi = hi + pd)
  list(naive = c(g$lo[g$grp == 1], g$hi[g$grp == 1]),
       rec   = c(g$lo[g$grp == 2], g$hi[g$grp == 2]))
}
padr <- function(r, f = .06) r + c(-1, 1) * diff(r) * f

# nice ratio breaks within [lo, hi] (RATIO units), ALWAYS including 1; dense
# (1-1.5-2-3-5-7) for narrow ranges, sparse (1-2-5) once it spans >~1 decade.
ratio_breaks <- function(lo, hi) {
  cand <- if (log10(hi) - log10(lo) <= 1.2)
            c(.1, .2, .3, .5, .7, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 15, 20, 30, 50, 70, 100)
          else c(.1, .2, .5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000)
  b <- cand[cand >= lo & cand <= hi]
  sort(unique(c(1, b)))
}

colhead <- function(k) ggplot() + theme_void() + ggtitle(ALAB[[k]], subtitle = FULL[[k]]) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = .5, size = 9),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = .5, size = 7))

# logr = TRUE -> log10 y-axis (Var/F ratios), breaks in ratio units incl 1, null at 1;
# logr = FALSE -> linear y-axis, breaks forced to include 0 (the null of row A).
deco <- function(p, col, split, xr, yl, yq, ttl = NULL, tag = NULL, bottom = FALSE, logr = FALSE) {
  ylim <- if (is.list(yl)) (if (col %in% naive) yl$naive else yl$rec) else yl
  if (logr) {
    ylim <- c(min(ylim[1], 1), max(ylim[2], 1))          # 1 must sit on the axis
    rb <- ratio_breaks(ylim[1], ylim[2])
    p <- p + scale_y_log10(breaks = rb, labels = as.character(rb)) +
      coord_cartesian(xlim = xr, ylim = ylim)
  } else {
    brk <- pretty(ylim); brk <- sort(unique(c(0, brk))); brk <- brk[brk >= ylim[1] & brk <= ylim[2]]
    p <- p + scale_y_continuous(breaks = brk) + coord_cartesian(xlim = xr, ylim = ylim)
  }
  if (col == "NR") p <- p + ylab(yq) + theme(axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8))
  else if (col == "R" && split) p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
  else p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                      axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
  # bottom row keeps the x numbers but NOT a per-panel title (one global "time (ms)" below)
  if (bottom) p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
  else p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())
  if (!is.null(tag)) p <- p + labs(tag = tag)
  if (!is.null(ttl)) p <- p + ggtitle(ttl) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8.5, face = "plain", margin = margin(b = 1)))
  p + theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), plot.margin = margin(1.5, 3, 1.5, 3))
}
base <- function() theme_bw(base_size = 8)

tr    <- range(tmap$t)
agon  <- tmap[agonist > 0]; x0 <- min(agon$t); x1 <- max(agon$t)
# full-panel agonist band. ymax=1e10 works on both scales; ymin branches because a log10
# axis rejects a negative floor (log10(<=0)=NaN, rect dropped) while a linear panel (score
# bias) goes negative and must reach the floor. coord_cartesian clips the ridiculous bounds.
mkshade <- function(logr = FALSE) annotate("rect", xmin = x0, xmax = x1,
                  ymin = if (logr) 1e-10 else -1e10, ymax = 1e10, fill = "grey85", alpha = .5)

# Row A: score bias, GROUPED (naive | recursive), null 0
yA <- grange(dA$mA - 1.96 * dA$seA, dA$mA + 1.96 * dA$seA, as.character(dA$algo))
pA <- lapply(ALGOS, function(a) {
  d <- dA[algo == a]
  g <- ggplot(d, aes(t, mA)) + mkshade() + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = NUL, linewidth = .4) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mA - 1.96 * seA, ymax = mA + 1.96 * seA), fill = COL, alpha = .25) +
    geom_line(colour = COL, linewidth = .4) + base()
  deco(g, a, split = TRUE, xr = tr, yl = yA, yq = expression(bar(s)[t]),
       ttl = if (a == "NR") "score bias (first moment)" else NULL, tag = if (a == "NR") "A" else NULL)
})
# Row B: per-interval Var(s)/F, SHARED log10, null 1 (robust scale via log-space quantiles:
# a stray near-zero-F interval must not set the axis)
yB <- 10^padr(log10(as.numeric(quantile(c(dB$rB_lo, dB$rB_hi), c(.01, .99), na.rm = TRUE))))
pB <- lapply(ALGOS, function(a) {
  d <- dB[algo == a]
  g <- ggplot(d, aes(t, rB)) + mkshade(TRUE) + geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = NUL, linewidth = .4) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = rB_lo, ymax = rB_hi), fill = COL, alpha = .25) +
    geom_line(colour = COL, linewidth = .4) + base()
  deco(g, a, split = FALSE, xr = tr, yl = yB, yq = expression(Var(s[t]) / F[t]),
       ttl = if (a == "NR") "per-interval information ratio" else NULL, tag = if (a == "NR") "B" else NULL,
       logr = TRUE)
})
# Row C: accumulated J_T/F_T, GROUPED (naive | recursive) log10, null 1, bottom -> time axis
# (grange in log space so the padded limits stay positive)
yC <- lapply(grange(log10(dC$rC_lo), log10(dC$rC_hi), as.character(dC$algo)), function(r) 10^r)
pC <- lapply(ALGOS, function(a) {
  d <- dC[algo == a]
  g <- ggplot(d, aes(t, rC)) + mkshade(TRUE) + geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = NUL, linewidth = .4) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = rC_lo, ymax = rC_hi), fill = COL, alpha = .25) +
    geom_line(colour = COL, linewidth = .4) + base()
  deco(g, a, split = TRUE, xr = tr, yl = yC, yq = expression(Var(sum(s[t])) / sum(F[t])),
       ttl = if (a == "NR") "accumulated information ratio" else NULL, tag = if (a == "NR") "C" else NULL,
       bottom = TRUE, logr = TRUE)
})

grid <- wrap_plots(c(lapply(ALGOS, colhead), pA, pB, pC), ncol = 5, heights = c(0.10, 1, 1, 1))
xlab_row <- wrap_elements(grid::textGrob("time (ms)", gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9)))   # one x title
g4 <- wrap_plots(grid, xlab_row, ncol = 1, heights = c(1, 0.03))
ggsave("Figure_4_score.pdf", g4, width = 7.0, height = 6.4)
```
